summary.intent: Address Codex AI review feedback on PR #679 (ars-ui) by fixing seven previously identified issues, pushing the fixes, and iterating through a second Codex review pass until the PR was clean.
summary.outcome: All seven original Codex threads were resolved and replied to. A second Codex pass identified one new valid bug (RTL horizontal menubar not opening with ArrowDown), which was fixed test-first with a regression test, passed clippy and the full MenuBar test suite, and was committed and pushed.

repo: The project uses a Codex AI reviewer triggered via '@codex review' PR comment; review state is tracked via GitHub reactions (eyes = in progress, thumbs-up = approved).
The repo is fogodev/ars-ui and uses Rust with nightly rustfmt and clippy -D warnings enforced in CI.
CI pipeline runs: Formatting, Snapshot Change Check, Clippy, Workspace Check, Unit Tests, Mutual Exclusion Guard — in roughly that order.
Menu bar keyboard navigation distinguishes text direction (LTR/RTL) and orientation (horizontal/vertical) for which arrow keys open top-level items.
The project uses a local skill at .agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md to define the Codex polling workflow.

code.finding: The match guard for horizontal ArrowDown activation was tied to `dir == Ltr`, incorrectly excluding RTL horizontal menubars. The fix splits the match arm so ArrowDown activates regardless of direction for horizontal orientation.

workflow: The Codex review loop: push fix → reply to all unresolved threads → resolve threads via GraphQL mutation → post '@codex review' comment → poll reactions and threads at ~2 minute intervals until terminal state.
Red/green cycle enforced before committing: regression test added and confirmed failing before applying the fix, then confirmed passing after.

Clippy's match_same_arms lint requires merging arms that share the same body, even when split for clarity — the unconditional activation patterns must be combined while keeping guarded variants separate.
nightly rustfmt must be run after any match arm restructuring, as stable fmt may not handle all patterns identically.

friction: Clippy flagged the initial match arm split as match_same_arms, requiring a second restructuring pass to satisfy both correctness and the linter simultaneously.
The Codex review polling loop requires ~2 minute sleep intervals and multiple round trips (reactions API + GraphQL threads API + PR checks), making the iteration cycle slow.
Pre-commit hooks extended the commit latency enough that the session had to wait before pushing.
open_items: CI was still running (Unit Tests and Mutual Exclusion Guard) at the point the transcript ends — final CI green status not confirmed.
The second Codex pass produced one thread that was addressed, but no confirmation that Codex issued a final approval (thumbs-up) after the third push was captured in the transcript.
